findings.reason: ac-4 minor: the blank-line collapse is a GLOBAL regex, so teardown normalizes every run of 3+ newlines anywhere in the user file, not only the seam left by the removed block. No text is lost (ac-4 'user content preserved' holds for text); only intentional multi-blank-line gaps in user content are squashed. Whitespace mutation, not content loss.
findings.suggested_fix: If exactness matters, collapse only at the removed-block seam (operate on the join point) instead of a file-wide replace. Optional.

findings.reason: Robustness: MANAGED_END has no leading newline and normalizeInstructionText does not guarantee a trailing newline, so a managedBody without a final newline would glue the end marker onto the last body line. Not a defect for shipped inputs — resources/managed/{CLAUDE,AGENTS}.md both end in 0x0a (verified via xxd), so the produced block is well-formed and round-trips.
findings.suggested_fix: Optional: ensure body ends with a newline before appending MANAGED_END to harden against future resources.

findings.location: resources/managed/CLAUDE.md vs resources/managed/AGENTS.md (identical, 11288 bytes each) and root CLAUDE.md charter
findings.reason: Duplication/drift risk: the two managed resources are byte-identical copies of the charter. Flagged as follow-up in the change notes. Confirmed it does not BREAK anything now — routing installs each independently and the managed-block markers carry per-file sha256, so divergence would be detected, not silently mismerged.
findings.suggested_fix: Track as a separate follow-up (single-source the charter into both resources at build time). Out of scope for this work item.

evidence.summary: 37 pass / 0 fail — covers ac-1..ac-5 incl. corruption (unbalanced/multiple markers preserve original), backup idempotency (FIRST original kept), GLOBAL_ routing, teardown strip+increment-preservation+backup-fallback+.ditto-kept+allowlist-removal.

evidence.command: bunx biome check (8 src + 5 test changed files)
evidence.summary: Checked 13 files, no fixes applied — clean.

0 type errors in any changed file. Pre-existing tsc errors exist only in unrelated test files (intent-quality-doctor, interview-driver, knowledge-bridge, run-with, pre-compact, autopilot-loop-drive, knowledge-record) — all in unmodified files.
exit 0; setup + teardown registered and described. CLI startup NOT broken by the new imports in src/cli/index.ts.

evidence.command: git stash push src/cli/index.ts scripts/install-plugin.mjs; bun test tests/doctor/permissions.test.ts; git stash pop
evidence.summary: 6 fail with tracked changes REVERTED to HEAD — identical to with-change run. Proves the doctor/bridge/e2e/source-identity subprocess failures (28 total in full run) are PRE-EXISTING/environmental, not caused by this change.
both end in 0x0a — buildManagedBlock produces well-formed blocks; no marker-glue defect for shipped inputs.

Conservative: empty file -> block+
; no trailing nl -> '

' separator; unbalanced (start-only/end-only) and multiple blocks -> 'corrupted' (original kept); apply refuses to write, teardown restores-from-backup-or-leaves. No user-content-DROP path found for the named edge cases.

pluginRoot=resolve(resourcesDir,'..','..')==projectRoot. Dev: repoRoot==projectRoot -> skip (no self-manage). Installed plugin: CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT != user project -> proceeds. No false-positive on user projects (their root never equals the plugin root).

unverified.reason: 28 of 1436 tests fail, but proven PRE-EXISTING via stash-revert (doctor/bridge subprocess + e2e-browser + source-repo-identity wi_v01bootstrap). None reference the changed managed-resource/setup/teardown surface; the 37 tests covering this change all pass.

unverified.item: Real installed-plugin run (CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT set, GLOBAL_ resource into ~/.claude)
unverified.reason: No GLOBAL_ resource is shipped (only CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md in resources/managed). GLOBAL_ routing is unit-tested with a synthetic GLOBAL_FOO.md but the end-to-end installed-plugin home-write is not exercised in this sandbox.
Repo does not typecheck-clean at HEAD (errors in 7 unrelated test files). Not introduced here; out of scope to fix.

recommended_next_action: Approve and merge. The change meets ac-1..ac-5 with green dedicated tests, clean biome/tsc on all changed files, and a verified-conservative corruption guard with no user-content-loss path. Track the resources/managed charter duplication as a separate follow-up.
